steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The verb "eschew" takes "currency" as its direct object, and "in favor of a direct trade system" is a prepositional phrase that completes the meaning of that object. These two elements form a unified expression and must not be separated by punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma after "currency": A comma separates "currency" from the phrase that defines what it's being exchanged for, fragmenting the meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — dash after "currency": A dash similarly breaks the tight connection between the object and its prepositional complement. ✗
  • Choice C — no punctuation between "currency" and "in favor of": Keeps the object and its prepositional phrase together, preserving the unified meaning. ✓
  • Choice D — comma or dash elsewhere in the phrase: Any inserted punctuation disrupts the flow of the integral prepositional phrase. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
